BMI 714 Final Project Rmd

Rohan Krishnamurthi

Initial Setup

# Load package(s)
library(tidymodels)
## ── Attaching packages ────────────────────────────────────── tidymodels 1.2.0 ──
## ✔ broom        1.0.7     ✔ recipes      1.1.0
## ✔ dials        1.3.0     ✔ rsample      1.2.1
## ✔ dplyr        1.1.4     ✔ tibble       3.2.1
## ✔ ggplot2      3.5.1     ✔ tidyr        1.3.1
## ✔ infer        1.0.7     ✔ tune         1.2.1
## ✔ modeldata    1.4.0     ✔ workflows    1.1.4
## ✔ parsnip      1.2.1     ✔ workflowsets 1.1.0
## ✔ purrr        1.0.2     ✔ yardstick    1.3.1
## ── Conflicts ───────────────────────────────────────── tidymodels_conflicts() ──
## ✖ purrr::discard() masks scales::discard()
## ✖ dplyr::filter()  masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()     masks stats::lag()
## ✖ recipes::step()  masks stats::step()
## • Use suppressPackageStartupMessages() to eliminate package startup messages
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ readr::col_factor() masks scales::col_factor()
## ✖ purrr::discard()    masks scales::discard()
## ✖ dplyr::filter()     masks stats::filter()
## ✖ stringr::fixed()    masks recipes::fixed()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()        masks stats::lag()
## ✖ readr::spec()       masks yardstick::spec()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(here)
## here() starts at /Users/rohankrishnamurthi/Downloads/BMI 714/Final Project/final-project-rohan
library(recipes)
library(xgboost)
## 
## Attaching package: 'xgboost'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     slice
library(doMC)
## Loading required package: foreach
## 
## Attaching package: 'foreach'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     accumulate, when
## 
## Loading required package: iterators
## Loading required package: parallel
# handle common conflicts
tidymodels_prefer()

# Set working directory
setwd("/Users/rohankrishnamurthi/Downloads/BMI 714/Final Project/final-project-rohan")

# Read in data tables
nhanes <- read_csv(("BMI714_NHANES2020_Data.csv")) |> 
  janitor::clean_names() 
## Warning: One or more parsing issues, call `problems()` on your data frame for details,
## e.g.:
##   dat <- vroom(...)
##   problems(dat)
## Rows: 15560 Columns: 1595
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr   (59): P_BPXO_BPAOARM, P_LUX_LUAPNME, P_OHXDEN_OHX02CTC, P_OHXDEN_OHX03...
## dbl (1454): SEQN, P_DEMO_SDDSRVYR, P_DEMO_RIDSTATR, P_DEMO_RIAGENDR, P_DEMO_...
## lgl   (82): P_BMX_BMIHEAD, P_OHXDEN_OHX02RTC, P_OHXDEN_OHX03RTC, P_OHXDEN_OH...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
nhanes_dictionary <- read_csv(("BMI714_NHANES_VariableDictionary.csv")) |> 
  janitor::clean_names() 
## Rows: 1595 Columns: 6
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (6): BMI_714_Variable_Name, Variable_Name, Variable_Description, Data_Fi...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Data Preparation

# AFTER SPLIT: do preprocessing of the training data, apply those same transformations to testing data

# Outcome variable, LDL: P_TRIGLY_LBDLDNSI

# Main Predictors (lab measurements)
# 1.    Triglycerides / VLDL: P_BIOPRO_LBXSTR
# 2.    Glucose: P_BIOPRO_LBXSGL
# 3.    Hemoglobin: P_CBC_LBXHGB
# 4.    Insulin: P_INS_LBXIN
# 5.    Liver func, Gamma Glutamyl Transferase (GGT): P_BIOPRO_LBXSGTSI
# 6.    Liver function, Alkaline Phosphatase (ALP): P_BIOPRO_LBXSAPSI
# 7.    Kidney function, creatinine, urine: P_ALB_CR_URXUCR
# 8.    C-reactive protein: P_HSCRP_LBXHSCRP
# 9.    White blood cell count (1000 cells/uL): P_CBC_LBXWBCSI

# Assess missingness
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_trigly_lbdldnsi))/nrow(nhanes) # 0.7
## [1] 0.701671
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_tchol_lbxtc))/nrow(nhanes) # 0.30
## [1] 0.3041131
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_trigly_lbxtr))/nrow(nhanes) # 0.70
## [1] 0.7011568
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_biopro_lbxstr))/nrow(nhanes) # 0.3911954
## [1] 0.3911954
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_biopro_lbxsgl))/nrow(nhanes) #0.39
## [1] 0.3911954
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_cbc_lbxhgb))/nrow(nhanes) #0.219
## [1] 0.2187661
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_ins_lbxin))/nrow(nhanes) #0.70
## [1] 0.7027635
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_biopro_lbxsatsi))/nrow(nhanes) #0.39
## [1] 0.3911954
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_biopro_lbxsassi))/nrow(nhanes)
## [1] 0.3936375
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_biopro_lbxsgtsi))/nrow(nhanes)
## [1] 0.3911954
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_biopro_lbxsapsi))/nrow(nhanes) #0.39
## [1] 0.3911311
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_alb_cr_urxucr))/nrow(nhanes) #0.196
## [1] 0.1960797
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_biopro_lbxscr))/nrow(nhanes) #0.39
## [1] 0.3910668
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_hscrp_lbxhscrp))/nrow(nhanes) #0.25
## [1] 0.253599
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_cbc_lbxwbcsi))/nrow(nhanes) # 0.2187661
## [1] 0.2187661
sum(is.na(nhanes$p_demo_ridreth3))/nrow(nhanes)
## [1] 0
# Select variables of interest (including potential confounders)
nhanes_filtered <- nhanes |> select(
  # seqn, #id
  p_trigly_lbdldnsi, #LDL, NIH eqn
  p_biopro_lbxstr, #Triglycerides
  p_biopro_lbxsgl, # Glucose
  p_cbc_lbxhgb, # Hemoglobin
  p_ins_lbxin, # Insulin, high missingness
  p_biopro_lbxsgtsi, # GGT
  p_biopro_lbxsapsi, # Alkaline Phosphatase
  p_alb_cr_urxucr, # Creatinine
  p_hscrp_lbxhscrp, # C-reactive protein
  p_cbc_lbxwbcsi, # White blood cell count
  p_demo_ridreth3, # Confounders for later
  p_demo_ridageyr, 
  p_demo_riagendr
)

# Only select complete rows with no missing values
nhanes_filtered_complete <- nhanes_filtered[complete.cases(nhanes_filtered), ]

# Filter even further
nhanes_filtered_subset <- nhanes_filtered_complete |> 
  select(
  # seqn,
  p_trigly_lbdldnsi, # LDL, NIH eqn
  p_biopro_lbxstr, #Triglycerides
  p_biopro_lbxsgl, # Glucose
  p_cbc_lbxhgb, # Hemoglobin
  p_ins_lbxin, # Insulin, high missingness
  p_biopro_lbxsgtsi, # GGT
  p_biopro_lbxsapsi, # Alkaline Phosphatase
  p_alb_cr_urxucr, # Creatinine
  p_hscrp_lbxhscrp, # C-reactive protein
  p_cbc_lbxwbcsi, # White blood cell count
  )

# Save data frames
save(nhanes_filtered_complete, file = ("data-splitting/nhanes_filtered_complete.rda"))
save(nhanes_filtered_subset, file = ("data-splitting/nhanes_filtered_subset.rda"))

Exploratory Data Analysis

# Make Plots


nhanes_filtered_subset %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -p_trigly_lbdldnsi, names_to = "predictor", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = p_trigly_lbdldnsi)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~ predictor, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(x = "Predictor value", y = "p_trigly_lbdldnsi", title = "LDL vs. Predictors") +
  theme_minimal()

# Check collinearity / correlation

cor_mat <- cor(nhanes_filtered_subset, use = "complete.obs")
print(cor_mat)
##                   p_trigly_lbdldnsi p_biopro_lbxstr p_biopro_lbxsgl
## p_trigly_lbdldnsi        1.00000000      0.22909979      0.03118821
## p_biopro_lbxstr          0.22909979      1.00000000      0.27187970
## p_biopro_lbxsgl          0.03118821      0.27187970      1.00000000
## p_cbc_lbxhgb             0.12377470      0.16165645      0.04574880
## p_ins_lbxin             -0.03533150      0.16464757      0.19369832
## p_biopro_lbxsgtsi        0.05329619      0.13387617      0.12483717
## p_biopro_lbxsapsi       -0.09680867     -0.03022774      0.06260028
## p_alb_cr_urxucr         -0.01105424     -0.05247759     -0.06515769
## p_hscrp_lbxhscrp         0.06411129      0.04994671      0.12898806
## p_cbc_lbxwbcsi           0.03027310      0.20155793      0.12113452
##                   p_cbc_lbxhgb p_ins_lbxin p_biopro_lbxsgtsi p_biopro_lbxsapsi
## p_trigly_lbdldnsi   0.12377470 -0.03533150        0.05329619      -0.096808671
## p_biopro_lbxstr     0.16165645  0.16464757        0.13387617      -0.030227744
## p_biopro_lbxsgl     0.04574880  0.19369832        0.12483717       0.062600280
## p_cbc_lbxhgb        1.00000000 -0.01759132        0.06766440      -0.013466264
## p_ins_lbxin        -0.01759132  1.00000000        0.10989897       0.070064562
## p_biopro_lbxsgtsi   0.06766440  0.10989897        1.00000000       0.091614026
## p_biopro_lbxsapsi  -0.01346626  0.07006456        0.09161403       1.000000000
## p_alb_cr_urxucr     0.08549213  0.02485871        0.02551053       0.027156018
## p_hscrp_lbxhscrp   -0.13175971  0.11608590        0.06920814       0.045649922
## p_cbc_lbxwbcsi      0.06532695  0.17205625        0.02581756       0.001844155
##                   p_alb_cr_urxucr p_hscrp_lbxhscrp p_cbc_lbxwbcsi
## p_trigly_lbdldnsi    -0.011054239       0.06411129    0.030273095
## p_biopro_lbxstr      -0.052477591       0.04994671    0.201557928
## p_biopro_lbxsgl      -0.065157686       0.12898806    0.121134525
## p_cbc_lbxhgb          0.085492127      -0.13175971    0.065326953
## p_ins_lbxin           0.024858711       0.11608590    0.172056252
## p_biopro_lbxsgtsi     0.025510529       0.06920814    0.025817560
## p_biopro_lbxsapsi     0.027156018       0.04564992    0.001844155
## p_alb_cr_urxucr       1.000000000       0.03129199    0.003406242
## p_hscrp_lbxhscrp      0.031291991       1.00000000    0.264791187
## p_cbc_lbxwbcsi        0.003406242       0.26479119    1.000000000
df_scaled <- scale(nhanes_filtered_subset |> select(-p_trigly_lbdldnsi))
kappa(df_scaled)
## [1] 1.956705
# Boxplot shows there is not great overlap in predictors' ranges; scaling is necessary
boxplot(nhanes_filtered_subset |> select(-p_trigly_lbdldnsi), main = "Boxplot of Predictors")

# OPTIONAL: SPLIT THE DATA
# Filtered Data
## Set seed for random split
set.seed(714)
nhanes_split <- nhanes_filtered_subset |> 
  initial_split(prop = 0.8, strata = p_trigly_lbdldnsi)

nhanes_train <- nhanes_split |> training()
nhanes_test <- nhanes_split |> testing()



##Use V-fold cross-validation (v = 5 folds, 3 repeats)
set.seed(714)
nhanes_folds <- vfold_cv(nhanes_train, v = 5, repeats = 3,
                      strata = p_trigly_lbdldnsi)


## Write out folds
save(nhanes_train, file = ("data-splitting/nhanes_1_train.rda"))
save(nhanes_test, file = ("data-splitting/nhanes_1_test.rda"))
save(nhanes_folds, file = ("data-splitting/nhanes_1_folds.rda"))


# SCALE/TRANSFORM DATA
# Scale Predictors
# Code categorical variables: not applicable
# Transform data


# Recipe scaling method
rec <- recipe(p_trigly_lbdldnsi ~ ., data = nhanes_train) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), one_hot = FALSE) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

rec_prep <- prep(rec, training = nhanes_train)
nhanes_train_scaled <- bake(rec_prep, new_data = nhanes_train)
nhanes_test_scaled  <- bake(rec_prep, new_data = nhanes_test)

# Other scaling method
X <- nhanes_train |> select(-p_trigly_lbdldnsi)

mu <- sapply(X, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
sd <- sapply(X, sd, na.rm = TRUE)

X_scaled <- scale(X, center = mu, scale = sd)

nhanes_train_scaled <- cbind(
  p_trigly_lbdldnsi = nhanes_train$p_trigly_lbdldnsi,
  as.data.frame(X_scaled)
)

Select and Run Regression Model

# MODEL: Boosted Tree, or Random Forest
# Variable Selection: not necessary
# Regularization: not necessary
# Recipe:
nhanes_recipe <- recipe(p_trigly_lbdldnsi ~ .,
                        data = nhanes_train) |> 
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

save(nhanes_recipe, file = ("recipes/nhanes_recipe.rda"))

# Run model 
# parallel processing to speed process up
num_cores <- parallel::detectCores(logical = TRUE)
registerDoMC(cores = num_cores)

# model specifications
bt_spec <- boost_tree(
  min_n = tune(),
  mtry = tune(),
  learn_rate = tune()
) |> 
  set_mode("regression") |> 
  set_engine("xgboost")

# define workflows ----
bt_wflow <- workflow() |> 
  add_model(bt_spec) |> 
  add_recipe(nhanes_recipe)

# hyperparameter tuning values ----
# Check ranges for hyperparameters
hardhat::extract_parameter_set_dials(bt_spec)
## Collection of 3 parameters for tuning
## 
##  identifier       type    object
##        mtry       mtry nparam[?]
##       min_n      min_n nparam[+]
##  learn_rate learn_rate nparam[+]
## 
## Model parameters needing finalization:
##    # Randomly Selected Predictors ('mtry')
## 
## See `?dials::finalize` or `?dials::update.parameters` for more information.
# Change hyperparameter ranges
bt_params <- hardhat::extract_parameter_set_dials(bt_spec) |> 
  update(mtry = mtry(c(1,10)), learn_rate = learn_rate(c(-5, -0.2)))

# Build tuning grid
bt_grid <- grid_regular(bt_params, levels = 5)

# fit workflows/models ----
set.seed(714)
bt_tuned <- bt_wflow |> 
  tune_grid(nhanes_folds, grid = bt_grid, control = control_grid(save_workflow = TRUE))

# Save results
save(bt_tuned, file = ("results/bt_tuned.rda"))

Evaluate model fit

# boosted tree model
bt_tuned |> collect_metrics()
## # A tibble: 250 × 9
##     mtry min_n learn_rate .metric .estimator   mean     n std_err .config       
##    <int> <int>      <dbl> <chr>   <chr>       <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>         
##  1     1     2    0.00001 rmse    standard   2.46      15 0.00558 Preprocessor1…
##  2     1     2    0.00001 rsq     standard   0.121     15 0.00594 Preprocessor1…
##  3     3     2    0.00001 rmse    standard   2.46      15 0.00558 Preprocessor1…
##  4     3     2    0.00001 rsq     standard   0.140     15 0.00508 Preprocessor1…
##  5     5     2    0.00001 rmse    standard   2.46      15 0.00558 Preprocessor1…
##  6     5     2    0.00001 rsq     standard   0.135     15 0.00494 Preprocessor1…
##  7     7     2    0.00001 rmse    standard   2.46      15 0.00558 Preprocessor1…
##  8     7     2    0.00001 rsq     standard   0.125     15 0.00501 Preprocessor1…
##  9    10     2    0.00001 rmse    standard   2.46      15 0.00558 Preprocessor1…
## 10    10     2    0.00001 rsq     standard   0.0990    15 0.00438 Preprocessor1…
## # ℹ 240 more rows
bt_tuned |> autoplot(metric = "rmse")

# Report key statistics
# Select best parameters
bt_metrics <- collect_metrics(bt_tuned) |> 
  filter(.metric == "rmse") |> 
  mutate(.config = "boosted tree") |> 
  rename(mean_rmse = mean)

best_bt_metrics <- bt_metrics |>
  filter(mean_rmse == min(mean_rmse))

best_bt_metrics
## # A tibble: 1 × 9
##    mtry min_n learn_rate .metric .estimator mean_rmse     n std_err .config     
##   <int> <int>      <dbl> <chr>   <chr>          <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>       
## 1     1    30      0.631 rmse    standard       0.897    15 0.00850 boosted tree
# Finalize workflow 
best_bt_wflow <- bt_tuned |> 
  extract_workflow(bt_tuned) |> 
  finalize_workflow(select_best(bt_tuned, metric = "rmse"))

# train final model
# set seed
set.seed(714)
bt_fit <- fit(best_bt_wflow, nhanes_train)

# Train Best Model on Entire dataset
# Finalize workflow 
best_bt_wflow <- bt_tuned |> 
  extract_workflow(bt_tuned) |> 
  finalize_workflow(select_best(bt_tuned, metric = "rmse"))

# train final model
# set seed
set.seed(714)
bt_fit <- fit(best_bt_wflow, nhanes_train)
bt_fit
## ══ Workflow [trained] ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## Preprocessor: Recipe
## Model: boost_tree()
## 
## ── Preprocessor ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## 1 Recipe Step
## 
## • step_normalize()
## 
## ── Model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## ##### xgb.Booster
## raw: 36.1 Kb 
## call:
##   xgboost::xgb.train(params = list(eta = 0.630957344480193, max_depth = 6, 
##     gamma = 0, colsample_bytree = 1, colsample_bynode = 0.111111111111111, 
##     min_child_weight = 30L, subsample = 1), data = x$data, nrounds = 15, 
##     watchlist = x$watchlist, verbose = 0, nthread = 1, objective = "reg:squarederror")
## params (as set within xgb.train):
##   eta = "0.630957344480193", max_depth = "6", gamma = "0", colsample_bytree = "1", colsample_bynode = "0.111111111111111", min_child_weight = "30", subsample = "1", nthread = "1", objective = "reg:squarederror", validate_parameters = "TRUE"
## xgb.attributes:
##   niter
## callbacks:
##   cb.evaluation.log()
## # of features: 9 
## niter: 15
## nfeatures : 9 
## evaluation_log:
##   iter training_rmse
##  <num>         <num>
##      1     1.2527070
##      2     0.9334887
##    ---           ---
##     14     0.7639987
##     15     0.7604588
# Finalize Predictions
final_predictions <- nhanes_test %>% 
  select(p_trigly_lbdldnsi) %>% 
  bind_cols(predict(bt_fit, nhanes_test)) |> 
  mutate(difference = abs(p_trigly_lbdldnsi - .pred))

  
# Assess model performance
get_metrics <- metric_set(rmse, rsq, mae)
pred_metrics <- as.data.frame(get_metrics(final_predictions, truth = p_trigly_lbdldnsi, estimate = .pred))
pred_metrics
##   .metric .estimator .estimate
## 1    rmse   standard 0.8598080
## 2     rsq   standard 0.1300211
## 3     mae   standard 0.6770105
# Plot Results
prediction_plot <- final_predictions |> ggplot(aes(x = p_trigly_lbdldnsi, y = .pred)) +
  geom_abline(color = "blue") + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) + 
  ylim(0,5) +
  labs(y = "Predicted p_trigly_lbdldnsi", x = "Actual p_trigly_lbdldnsi",
       title = "Original Boosted Tree Model Predictions")

prediction_plot

mean(nhanes_filtered_complete$p_trigly_lbdldnsi)
## [1] 2.767295

Alternative Model

# Dataset including Confounder variables:
nhanes_filtered_complete <- nhanes_filtered_complete |> 
  mutate(p_demo_ridreth3 = as.factor(p_demo_ridreth3),
         p_demo_riagendr = as.factor(p_demo_riagendr))

# SPLIT THE DATA
set.seed(715)
nhanes_split_2 <- nhanes_filtered_complete |> 
  initial_split(prop = 0.8, strata = p_trigly_lbdldnsi)

nhanes_train_2 <- nhanes_split_2 |> training()
nhanes_test_2 <- nhanes_split_2 |> testing()

# Use V-fold cross-validation (v = 5 folds, 3 repeats)
set.seed(714)
nhanes_folds_2 <- vfold_cv(nhanes_train_2, v = 5, repeats = 3, strata = p_trigly_lbdldnsi)

# Recipe
nhanes_recipe_2 <- recipe(p_trigly_lbdldnsi ~ .,
                        data = nhanes_train_2) |> 
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), one_hot = FALSE) |> 
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())


# Run model 
# parallel processing to speed process up
num_cores <- parallel::detectCores(logical = TRUE)
registerDoMC(cores = num_cores)

# model specifications
bt_spec_2 <- boost_tree(
  min_n = tune(),
  mtry = tune(),
  learn_rate = tune()
) |> 
  set_mode("regression") |> 
  set_engine("xgboost")

# define workflows ----
bt_wflow_2 <- workflow() |> 
  add_model(bt_spec_2) |> 
  add_recipe(nhanes_recipe_2)

# Change hyperparameter ranges
bt_params_2 <- hardhat::extract_parameter_set_dials(bt_spec_2) |> 
  update(mtry = mtry(c(1,10)), learn_rate = learn_rate(c(-5, -0.2)))

# Build tuning grid
bt_grid_2 <- grid_regular(bt_params_2, levels = 5)

# fit workflows/models ----
set.seed(714)
bt_tuned_2 <- bt_wflow |> 
  tune_grid(nhanes_folds_2, grid = bt_grid_2, control = control_grid(save_workflow = TRUE))

# Save results
save(bt_tuned_2, file = ("results/bt_tuned_2.rda"))


# Evaluate Model Fit

# boosted tree model
bt_tuned_2 |> collect_metrics()
## # A tibble: 250 × 9
##     mtry min_n learn_rate .metric .estimator  mean     n std_err .config        
##    <int> <int>      <dbl> <chr>   <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>          
##  1     1     2    0.00001 rmse    standard   2.45     15 0.00437 Preprocessor1_…
##  2     1     2    0.00001 rsq     standard   0.130    15 0.00635 Preprocessor1_…
##  3     3     2    0.00001 rmse    standard   2.45     15 0.00437 Preprocessor1_…
##  4     3     2    0.00001 rsq     standard   0.147    15 0.00581 Preprocessor1_…
##  5     5     2    0.00001 rmse    standard   2.45     15 0.00437 Preprocessor1_…
##  6     5     2    0.00001 rsq     standard   0.143    15 0.00525 Preprocessor1_…
##  7     7     2    0.00001 rmse    standard   2.45     15 0.00437 Preprocessor1_…
##  8     7     2    0.00001 rsq     standard   0.135    15 0.00476 Preprocessor1_…
##  9    10     2    0.00001 rmse    standard   2.45     15 0.00437 Preprocessor1_…
## 10    10     2    0.00001 rsq     standard   0.101    15 0.00497 Preprocessor1_…
## # ℹ 240 more rows
bt_tuned_2 |> autoplot(metric = "rmse")

# Report key statistics
# Select best parameters
bt_metrics_2 <- collect_metrics(bt_tuned_2) |> 
  filter(.metric == "rmse") |> 
  mutate(.config = "boosted tree") |> 
  rename(mean_rmse = mean)

best_bt_metrics_2 <- bt_metrics_2 |>
  filter(mean_rmse == min(mean_rmse))

best_bt_metrics_2
## # A tibble: 1 × 9
##    mtry min_n learn_rate .metric .estimator mean_rmse     n std_err .config     
##   <int> <int>      <dbl> <chr>   <chr>          <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>       
## 1     1    40      0.631 rmse    standard       0.888    15 0.00561 boosted tree

Follow-up Analysis: Accuracy of Prediction in Held-Out Data

# ORIGINAL MODEL
# Train Best Model on Entire dataset
# Finalize workflow 
best_bt_wflow <- bt_tuned |> 
  extract_workflow(bt_tuned) |> 
  finalize_workflow(select_best(bt_tuned, metric = "rmse"))

# train final model
# set seed
set.seed(714)
bt_fit <- fit(best_bt_wflow, nhanes_train)
bt_fit
## ══ Workflow [trained] ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## Preprocessor: Recipe
## Model: boost_tree()
## 
## ── Preprocessor ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## 1 Recipe Step
## 
## • step_normalize()
## 
## ── Model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## ##### xgb.Booster
## raw: 36.1 Kb 
## call:
##   xgboost::xgb.train(params = list(eta = 0.630957344480193, max_depth = 6, 
##     gamma = 0, colsample_bytree = 1, colsample_bynode = 0.111111111111111, 
##     min_child_weight = 30L, subsample = 1), data = x$data, nrounds = 15, 
##     watchlist = x$watchlist, verbose = 0, nthread = 1, objective = "reg:squarederror")
## params (as set within xgb.train):
##   eta = "0.630957344480193", max_depth = "6", gamma = "0", colsample_bytree = "1", colsample_bynode = "0.111111111111111", min_child_weight = "30", subsample = "1", nthread = "1", objective = "reg:squarederror", validate_parameters = "TRUE"
## xgb.attributes:
##   niter
## callbacks:
##   cb.evaluation.log()
## # of features: 9 
## niter: 15
## nfeatures : 9 
## evaluation_log:
##   iter training_rmse
##  <num>         <num>
##      1     1.2527070
##      2     0.9334887
##    ---           ---
##     14     0.7639987
##     15     0.7604588
# Finalize Predictions
final_predictions <- nhanes_test %>% 
  select(p_trigly_lbdldnsi) %>% 
  bind_cols(predict(bt_fit, nhanes_test)) |> 
  mutate(difference = abs(p_trigly_lbdldnsi - .pred))

  
# Assess model performance
get_metrics <- metric_set(rmse, rsq, mae)
pred_metrics <- as.data.frame(get_metrics(final_predictions, truth = p_trigly_lbdldnsi, estimate = .pred))
pred_metrics
##   .metric .estimator .estimate
## 1    rmse   standard 0.8598080
## 2     rsq   standard 0.1300211
## 3     mae   standard 0.6770105
# Plot Results
prediction_plot <- final_predictions |> ggplot(aes(x = p_trigly_lbdldnsi, y = .pred)) +
  geom_abline(color = "blue") + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) + 
  ylim(0,5) +
  labs(y = "Predicted p_trigly_lbdldnsi", x = "Actual p_trigly_lbdldnsi")

prediction_plot

# NEW MODEL
# Finalize workflow 
best_bt_wflow_2 <- bt_tuned_2 |> 
  extract_workflow(bt_tuned_2) |> 
  finalize_workflow(select_best(bt_tuned_2, metric = "rmse"))

# train final model
# set seed
set.seed(714)
bt_fit_2 <- fit(best_bt_wflow_2, nhanes_train_2)

# Finalize Predictions
final_predictions_2 <- nhanes_test_2 |> 
  select(p_trigly_lbdldnsi) |> 
  bind_cols(predict(bt_fit_2, nhanes_test_2)) |> 
  mutate(difference = abs(p_trigly_lbdldnsi - .pred))

# Assess model performance
get_metrics_2 <- metric_set(rmse, rsq, mae)
pred_metrics_2 <- as.data.frame(get_metrics_2(final_predictions_2, truth = p_trigly_lbdldnsi, estimate = .pred))
pred_metrics_2
##   .metric .estimator .estimate
## 1    rmse   standard 0.8721142
## 2     rsq   standard 0.1295135
## 3     mae   standard 0.6734737
# WHICH MODEL PERFORMS BETTER
# Hypothesis test comparing the two
vec1 <- final_predictions$difference
vec2 <- final_predictions_2$difference

t.test(x = vec1, y = vec2, alternative = "greater")
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  vec1 and vec2
## t = 0.13868, df = 1804.4, p-value = 0.4449
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is greater than 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.03843273         Inf
## sample estimates:
## mean of x mean of y 
## 0.6770105 0.6734737
# p = 0.4449, inconclusive if adding confounders made model stronger

# Fail to reject null
